message:
fix(claudeconfig): prevent WriteModel panic on JSON null settings

Fourth-pass review caught an actual crash-severity bug: if
settings.json contains literal JSON \`null\` (valid JSON that Go
unmarshals by setting the destination map to nil, not an error),
then WriteModel's subsequent \`parsed[ModelKey] = model\` assignment
hits the classic Go panic "assignment to entry in nil map" and
crashes whatever process is doing the write.

The trigger path:
  1. User (or something) writes literal \`null\` into settings.json.
  2. WriteModel reads it, existed=true, len(raw)>0, unmarshal into
     the pre-initialized parsed map succeeds but replaces parsed
     with nil.
  3. \`parsed[ModelKey] = model\` → PANIC.

Fix: after a successful unmarshal, reset parsed to an empty
non-nil map if it came back nil. JSON \`null\` now behaves
semantically like "no pre-existing object to merge with" — the
subsequent write replaces the \`null\` content with either \`{}\`
(clear-model path) or \`{"model": "..."}\` (set-model path),
matching what the user would expect had the file been empty.

TestWriteModelHandlesJSONNullSettings exercises both sides:
  - Setting a model on null-content file succeeds and produces
    a normal {"model": "X"} file.
  - Clearing the model on null-content file produces "{}".
  - Neither panics.

Reads were already safe (Go map reads on nil return zero+false,
no panic). The \`removeIfEmptyObject\` helper added in the third
pass also guards against nil maps explicitly. WriteModel was the
last remaining assignment-into-parsed-map site without the guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
